expectations.text: Identifies AC-2(3) as the Automated/Disable Accounts control and explains what it requires
expectations.evidence: The response opens with 'Control: AC-2(3) — Account Management | Disable Accounts' and quotes the full control statement: 'Disable accounts within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when the accounts: (a) Have expired; (b) Are no longer associated with a user...' It explicitly ties the ODV of 90 days to the finding of 120 accounts inactive for 120+ days.

expectations.text: Recommends immediately disabling the 120 identified inactive accounts
expectations.evidence: Track A (Immediate Operational Remediation Days 1-7) explicitly states 'PRIORITY 1 (Day 2): Disable all privileged/admin accounts in the inactive set first' and 'PRIORITY 2 (Day 3-4): Disable all standard user accounts confirmed inactive for > 90 days.' Milestone 2 and 3 in the POA&M also mandate disablement within days 3 and 5 of SAR receipt.

expectations.text: Provides POA&M required fields including control ID, risk level, milestone steps, and completion date
expectations.evidence: Part 3 provides a complete POA&M table including: Control Identifier (AC-2(3)), Risk Level (MODERATE with rationale), Scheduled Completion Date (60 days with FedRAMP 180-day reference), Responsible Parties, and nine numbered milestones with owners, target dates, and evidence artifacts.

expectations.text: Advises fixing the automated account review/enforcement process
expectations.evidence: Step 6 (Track B) is titled 'Automated Controls Implementation (Day 14-45)' and directs: 'Configure Active Directory... to automatically flag accounts reaching 75 days of inactivity... Configure automatic disablement at 90 days of inactivity... Establish automated notifications to account managers at 60 days and 75 days.' Milestone 6 in the POA&M also targets this: 'Implement or repair automated account inactivity monitoring and disablement at the 90-day threshold.'

expectations.text: Notes that OTS findings must be tracked until closure and mentions ISSO/System Owner accountability
expectations.evidence: Part 4 ('ISSO AND SYSTEM OWNER SIGN-OFF REQUIREMENTS') explicitly states ISSO and System Owner sign-off requirements at each stage. The POA&M Status field notes 'OPEN [at time of entry] — Update monthly per ConMon requirements.' Milestone 9 is 'POA&M Closure and ISSO/SO Sign-Off', and Part 7 checklist tracks the item until submission to AO/AODR.
